github_access_strategy.overview: This file defines how the devops-concert-shell mode must interact with GitHub when running inside Bob Shell on GitHub Actions. The primary objective is to make GitHub MCP the default integration path while preserving workflow reliability through controlled fallback behavior.

primary_principle.rule: Use GitHub MCP first for every supported GitHub operation.
primary_principle.fallback_rule: Use GitHub CLI or authenticated GitHub REST API only when the GitHub MCP path fails, is unavailable, or returns incomplete data that blocks completion.
primary_principle.never_rule: Never prefer `gh` over GitHub MCP when MCP can perform the action successfully.

operation.name: fetch_issue_details

allowed_mcp_patterns.pattern: fetch a specific issue by number
fetch a specific issue by full GitHub issue URL
use a mode-provided GitHub issue fetch abstraction when available

operation.forbidden_assumption: Do not assume a specific raw MCP tool name such as `get_issue` is exposed in Bob Shell
operation.fallback: gh issue view or GitHub REST API

operation.name: list_assigned_issues
operation.fallback: gh issue list or GitHub REST API

operation.name: create_branch
operation.fallback: local git checkout -b plus git push -u origin

operation.name: create_pull_request
operation.preferred_path: GitHub MCP pull request creation capability
operation.fallback: gh pr create or GitHub REST API

operation.name: label_pull_request
operation.preferred_path: GitHub MCP PR or issue update capability when exposed
operation.fallback: gh pr edit or GitHub REST API

operation.name: request_reviewers
operation.preferred_path: GitHub MCP reviewer request capability when exposed
operation.fallback: gh pr edit or GitHub REST API

operation.name: add_comment
operation.preferred_path: GitHub MCP comment capability when exposed
operation.fallback: gh issue comment or GitHub REST API

shell_constraints.constraint: Bob Shell execution is non-interactive, so the prompt must not depend on follow-up questions to complete GitHub operations.
The prompt should contain the issue number, repository, and expected automation phase whenever possible.
GitHub MCP failures must be treated as recoverable unless the task is impossible without repository access.

step.action: Attempt the GitHub operation with MCP first

examples.example: Fetch issue details using the shell-available GitHub issue retrieval path
Create branch with the shell-available GitHub branch creation path
Create pull request with the shell-available GitHub PR creation path

step.critical_note: In Bob Shell, do not narrate or assume a specific MCP tool name unless that exact tool is known to be available in the current mode runtime.

success_conditions.condition: The needed GitHub object was created or retrieved successfully
Required metadata such as issue number, branch name, PR number, or PR URL is present

fallback_triggers.trigger: MCP returns authentication or transport failure
MCP returns incomplete data that prevents the workflow from continuing
MCP does not expose the specific GitHub action needed

step.action: Document the fallback in the completion summary
step.requirement: If fallback was used, state which step needed fallback and why.

issue_handling.item: Use the shell-available GitHub MCP issue retrieval path to read the triggering issue
If the prompt includes a full GitHub issue URL, prefer the MCP path that accepts the URL directly
Use GitHub MCP search or listing only when necessary for issue discovery
Avoid broad repository issue listing if the prompt already specifies the issue
Never hard-code `get_issue` as the required MCP tool name in instructions or reasoning

branch_handling.item: Prefer GitHub MCP create_branch when creating the remediation branch remotely
After remote branch creation, use local git checkout/fetch to work on that branch in the workspace
If MCP branch creation fails, create the branch locally and push it

pr_handling.item: Prefer GitHub MCP create_pull_request for PR creation
Extract the PR number and PR URL directly from the MCP response
Use the human-facing PR URL in the final completion summary whenever available

parseable_output_requirements.rule: The completion output must be stable and easy for GitHub Actions to parse.

anti_pattern.name: default_to_gh
anti_pattern.description: Using `gh` for routine issue and PR operations before trying GitHub MCP
anti_pattern.correct_approach: Attempt MCP first and fallback only on failure

anti_pattern.name: interactive_discovery
anti_pattern.description: Asking follow-up questions to discover the issue during GitHub Actions execution
anti_pattern.correct_approach: Use the issue number, URL, or repository context already provided by the prompt or environment

anti_pattern.name: non_parseable_completion
anti_pattern.description: Returning only prose without explicit PR metadata fields
anti_pattern.correct_approach: Emit stable uppercase fields for PR number, URL, branch, package, and issue number

validation_checklist.item: GitHub MCP was attempted first for supported GitHub operations
Fallback was used only when needed
The triggering issue was resolved using repository-specific context
The final output includes PR_NUMBER and PR_URL
The output states whether MCP was primary-only or fallback-assisted
